label: US Troops Deployed in Europe
contestNote: Partial drawdown from eastern flank announced Oct 2025; Poland and Lithuania say not notified

label: EU ReArm Europe Defense Fund
source: European Commission, ReArm Europe Plan, Mar 2025
contestNote: €650B is fiscal flexibility for member states, not direct EU spending; €150B is the binding SAFE loan instrument

label: Germany Defense Spending (% GDP)
deltaNote: First time Germany met 2% target since post-Cold War era; projected 3.5% by 2029

label: NATO Agreed Spending Target (by 2035)
contestNote: 3.5% for core defense + 1.5% for infrastructure/resilience; Spain and Belgium initially resisted

label: NATO Total Annual Defense Spending
deltaNote: Increase from 2023; represents ~55% of global military expenditure

label: Estimated European NATO Underinvestment Since 2014
contestNote: Methodology disputed; based on gap between actual spending and 2% target across all European members since Wales Pledge

label: NATO Member States
deltaNote: Finland (Apr 2023) and Sweden (Mar 2024) joined — largest expansion since 2004

label: Germany Special Defense Fund (Sondervermögen)
deltaNote: Original €100B fund from 2022 Zeitenwende expanded to €500B via constitutional amendment

label: US Share of NATO Defense Spending
contestNote: US counts Indo-Pacific and global commitments in spending; European analysts argue effective European contribution to European defense is higher
